task_list.title: Phase 1: Read and restate the issue
task_list.notes: Issue: NVIDIADriver validation checks overlap globally across CR node selectors. Custom CRs blocked by a default no-selector CR exit reconcile without a retry. Deleting/updating that conflicting CR changes global state, but peer CRs are not requeued, so they stay stuck until another event occurs. Repro: install default CR, create multiple custom selector CRs, label matching nodes, observe conflict, delete default CR, peers do not proceed. Fix should minimally trigger peer NVIDIADriver reconciliation on changes that can affect global conflict validation, without modifying tests.

task_list.title: Phase 2: Inspect repository guidance and test commands
task_list.notes: No AGENTS.md present. Makefile unit-test target builds then runs go test across non-e2e packages. Relevant baseline `go test ./controllers` passes.

task_list.title: Phase 3: Explore relevant controller code
task_list.notes: Relevant files: controllers/nvidiadriver_controller.go (Reconcile validates selectors and SetupWithManager registers watches), internal/validator/validator.go (global nodeSelector conflict validation), api/nvidia/v1alpha1/nvidiadriver_types.go (default no-selector maps to nvidia.com/gpu.present=true). Most likely fix location is the NVIDIADriver primary watch in SetupWithManager, previously using TypedEnqueueRequestForObject so peer CRs were not queued on another CR's delete/update.

task_list.title: Phase 4: Create temporary reproduction script
task_list.notes: Created /tmp/repro_nvidiadriver_peer_requeue.py. It failed on original code because the primary NVIDIADriver watch used TypedEnqueueRequestForObject. After fix, extended edge checks passed and the temp script was removed. Later terminal session dropped before rerunning the static script again, but Go tests were rerun after the typed-nil adjustment.

task_list.title: Phase 5: Analyze fix location and approach
task_list.notes: Replace the primary NVIDIADriver watch handler with a map handler that includes the event object and all current NVIDIADriver resources. Keep GenerationChangedPredicate to avoid status-only update loops. Reuse a helper to list all NVIDIADriver reconcile requests and dedupe names. This requeues peers when any NVIDIADriver create/update/delete can alter global conflict state.

task_list.title: Phase 6: Implement minimal fix
task_list.notes: Added nvidiaDriverReconcileRequests helper and switched the primary NVIDIADriver watch to TypedEnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc. Existing ClusterPolicy/Node fan-out now reuse the helper. Added nil guard before adding source to avoid typed-nil interface panic for non-driver fan-out callers.

task_list.title: Phase 7: Verify fix
task_list.notes: Temporary reproduction script passed with edge checks before cleanup. Ran `go test ./controllers` and `go test ./internal/validator ./api/nvidia/v1alpha1`; all passed. After typed-nil guard, reran `go test ./controllers ./internal/validator ./api/nvidia/v1alpha1`; all passed.

task_list.title: Phase 8: Final review against base commit
task_list.notes: Reviewed final source via diff/file viewer. Only non-test file intentionally modified: controllers/nvidiadriver_controller.go. No test files were modified. There is an unrelated untracked reports/ directory visible in git status that was not touched.

action.message:
Implemented the NVIDIADriver peer requeue fix in `/workspace/gpu-operator/`.

Summary:
- Root cause: `controllers/nvidiadriver_controller.go` watched `NVIDIADriver` CRs with `TypedEnqueueRequestForObject`, so deleting/updating a conflicting CR only reconciled that CR. Blocked peer `NVIDIADriver` resources were not requeued to re-run global nodeSelector validation.
- Fix: added `nvidiaDriverReconcileRequests`, which builds deduplicated reconcile requests for:
  - the current event object, when present, preserving existing direct reconciliation behavior;
  - all current `NVIDIADriver` resources, so peers are requeued when global conflict state changes.
- Updated the primary `NVIDIADriver` watch to use `TypedEnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc` with that helper.
- Reused the same helper for existing `ClusterPolicy` and `Node` fan-out watches.
- Kept `TypedGenerationChangedPredicate` on the `NVIDIADriver` watch, so status-only updates remain filtered and should not cause reconcile loops.
- Added a nil guard for non-driver fan-out callers to avoid typed-nil interface panics.

Files changed:
- `controllers/nvidiadriver_controller.go`

Verification performed:
- Created a temporary reproduction/edge-check script that failed before the fix and passed after the watch changed to peer requeue behavior; removed the script afterward.
- Ran `go test ./controllers` successfully.
- Ran `go test ./internal/validator ./api/nvidia/v1alpha1` successfully.
- After the nil-guard refinement, reran `go test ./controllers ./internal/validator ./api/nvidia/v1alpha1` successfully.

I did not modify any test files. I also noticed an unrelated untracked `reports/` directory in `git status`; I left it untouched.
